title: UnivOrch definition document
description: A relative tree of folders and VMs that the 'load' command attaches to a destination folder. Folders are keys ending in '/'; VMs are keys without '/'. The destination's own properties are never modified by load. Resources (hypervisors, VM templates) and imports belong inside a folder definition, never at the document root.

Folder.description: A folder to create inside the destination. May contain its own description, declared resources (hypervisors, VM templates), an explicit import list, nested folders (keys ending in '/') and VMs (keys without '/').

oneOf.description: Special value 'ALL' imports everything visible at the parent level.
List of resource names to import from the parent (supports '*' wildcards).

define hypervisors.description: Hypervisors declared in this folder, keyed by name.

define machine templates.description: VM templates declared in this folder, keyed by name.

Hypervisor.description: A hypervisor declared inside a folder. Carries the connector type ('mock', 'vmware', 'proxmox') and free-text description; address and credentials come in future sprints.

type.description: Connector type (must be a registered connector).

VMTemplate.description: A VM template declared inside a folder. Same shape as a Descriptor body but cannot itself reference another template (use 'based on:' in a future sprint for derivation).

use hypervisor.description: Name of a declared hypervisor accessible from this folder.

base_vm.description: Name of the base VM on the hypervisor to clone from.

Descriptor.description: A VM to create. May reference a template via 'use template:' and a hypervisor via 'use hypervisor:'. Fields are optional because they may be inherited from a template (the orchestrator validates the final, resolved shape).

use hypervisor.description: Name of a declared hypervisor accessible from this VM's folder.

base_vm.description: Name of the base VM on the hypervisor to clone from.

use template.description: Name of a VM template to inherit fields from.
